ingredients.name: Niimura Main Store
ingredients.localName: とんかつ にいむら 本店
ingredients.address: Niimura Main Store, 1 Chome-23-10 Kabukicho, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This long-established tonkatsu restaurant in Kabukicho, Shinjuku served as a background reference location in Episode 4 of Season 2, with its distinctive shopfront appearing in the show's depiction of Z-City's urban dining district. Fans of the series recognize it as one of several real Shinjuku-area eateries woven into the show's streetscapes.

ingredients.name: Sushizanmai Koenji
ingredients.localName: すしざんまい 高円寺店
ingredients.address: Sushizanmai Koenji, 富士建設ビル 1F 4 Chome-26-15 Koenjiminami, Suginami City, Tokyo 166-0003, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Koenji branch of the popular sushi chain Sushizanmai appears as a background location in Episode 4 of Season 2, with the Koenji area serving as a key real-world reference for the everyday commercial streets that define Z-City's shopping district. Its bright signage is one of the recognizable street-level details fans spot on pilgrimage.

ingredients.name: 7-Eleven Koenji Ekimae Store
ingredients.localName: セブン-イレブン 高円寺駅前店
ingredients.address: 7-Eleven, 小野田ビル 1F 4 Chome-26-5 Koenjiminami, Suginami City, Tokyo 166-0003, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This 7-Eleven just outside Koenji Station's south exit is a confirmed pilgrimage spot from Episode 5 of Season 2, where the surrounding streetscape was used as a real-world reference for Z-City's commercial blocks. Convenience stores like this one are a recurring motif in Saitama's mundane daily life, making it a fun stop for fans walking the Koenji pilgrimage route.

ingredients.name: Kuriningu Tanpopo Koenji Minamiten
ingredients.localName: クリーニング たんぽぽ 高円寺南店
ingredients.address: Kuriningu Tanpopo Koenji Minamiten, 高円寺パレスハイツ 4 Chome-27-2 Koenjiminami, Suginami City, Tokyo 166-0003, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small dry cleaner on Koenji's south side appears in Episode 5 of Season 2 as one of the ordinary neighborhood storefronts that give Z-City its lived-in residential character. The Koenji south exit area was used extensively by the production team as a real-world model for the slice-of-life backgrounds around where Saitama shops and walks.

ingredients.name: McDonald's Musashisakai North Exit
ingredients.localName: マクドナルド 武蔵境北口店
ingredients.address: McDonald's Musashisakai North Exit, 1 Chome-2-26 Sakai, Musashino, Tokyo 180-0022, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The McDonald's at Musashisakai Station's north exit was used as a background location reference in Episode 6 of Season 2, with the busy station-front plaza feeding into the show's depiction of Z-City's commercial hubs. The Musashisakai area in Musashino City contributed several real-world street references to Season 2's urban backgrounds.

ingredients.name: Myojin-shita Kandagawa Main Store
ingredients.localName: 明神下 神田川 本店
ingredients.address: Myojin-shita Kandagawa Main Store, 2 Chome-5-11 Sotokanda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 101-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This historic unagi restaurant near Kanda Myojin shrine is a confirmed Season 2 pilgrimage spot, appearing in Episode 7 as a backdrop for a scene involving the Hero Association's world of affluence and power. The restaurant's century-old atmosphere and the Sotokanda neighborhood around it served as a refined counterpoint to Saitama's ordinary Z-City life.

ingredients.name: Shibuya Scramble Crossing
ingredients.localName: 渋谷スクランブル交差点
ingredients.entityContext: The world-famous Shibuya Scramble Crossing is featured in Episode 8 of Season 2 as a large crowd scene backdrop, conveying the chaotic scale of Z-City when monster threats begin escalating and civilians swarm the streets. It is one of the most instantly recognizable real-world Tokyo landmarks to appear in the series.

ingredients.name: Hachiko Memorial Statue
ingredients.entityContext: The iconic Hachiko statue at Shibuya Station's west exit is a confirmed Season 2 pilgrimage spot appearing in Episode 4, where the busy plaza around it stands in for a crowded city meeting point. The statue and the surrounding pedestrian plaza are among the most recognized real-world Tokyo backdrops used in Season 2.

ingredients.name: Repark Matsubara 1-Chome #2
ingredients.localName: 三井のリパーク 松原１丁目第２駐車場
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-39-15 Matsubara, Setagaya City, Tokyo 156-0043, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This coin parking lot in Matsubara, Setagaya appears in Episode 2 of Season 2 and sits in the heart of the real-world neighborhood that models Saitama's Z-City. The Matsubara 1-chome area around Meidaimae Station is the most documented One Punch Man pilgrimage zone in Tokyo, and this parking lot is one of the street-level details fans match directly to anime frames.

ingredients.name: Musashisakai Station
ingredients.address: Musashisakai Station, 2 Chome-1-12 Kyonancho, Musashino, Tokyo 180-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Musashisakai Station, a busy JR Chuo Line and Seibu Tama-gawa Line hub in Musashino City, appears as a background location in Episode 5 of Season 2. The station's plaza and surrounding commercial streets contributed to the show's depiction of Z-City's transit nodes, and the area is part of the broader west-Tokyo corridor that inspired One Punch Man's urban settings.

ingredients.name: BAR HICOTTO
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒156-0043 Tokyo, Setagaya City, Matsubara, 2 Chome−39−12 B1F BAR HICOTTO
ingredients.entityContext: This basement bar on Matsubara 2-chome in Setagaya is a confirmed Episode 2 pilgrimage spot, located just a few blocks from the Via Meidaimae apartment building that inspired Saitama's own home. The entire Matsubara neighborhood in Setagaya is the real-world heart of Z-City, and BAR HICOTTO's surrounding streetscape appears in the opening episodes of Season 2 as a background for scenes set near Saitama's home turf.
